acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] `BeadSnapshotWireV1` and `BeadPatchWireV1` exist in `beads-core`, and all snapshot/patch JSON encoding uses them.
- [ ] There is exactly one semantic validator for bead patches, and all mutation/event paths rely on it.
- [ ] `crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs` no longer defines a competing bead snapshot wire; it adapts to the canonical wire.
- [ ] Checkpoint import/export uses the canonical snapshot wire for parsing/serialization.
- [ ] Duplicate validators (ParsedBeadPatch/validate_wire_patch) are removed or reduced to input normalization only.
- [ ] Unit tests cover roundtrip snapshot serialization and invalid patch rejection through the canonical validator.
description:
**Problem**
Bead state is serialized and validated in multiple parallel shapes:
- Git store wire in `crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs`
- Checkpoint/realtime wire in `crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs`
- Patch validation in `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs` (ValidatedBeadPatch)
- Patch validation + parsing in `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs`
- Surface patch shape in `crates/beads-surface/src/ops.rs`

The same invariants (workflow/claim coherence, required fields, ordering rules, dedup, etc.) are re-encoded in different representations and checked in different places. This is scatter + drift risk: anyone changing bead semantics has to chase multiple pipelines and keep them in sync.

**Files:**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs`
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ops.rs`
design:
**Design**
Create one canonical bead snapshot wire and one canonical bead patch wire in `beads-core`, plus a single validator that yields a validated patch type. Then route all serialization and mutation pipelines through those types.

Concrete plan:
1) Define `BeadSnapshotWireV1` and `BeadPatchWireV1` in `crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs` (or a new `wire_bead_snapshot.rs`), covering all fields currently represented across git/checkpoint/event wires. These types must be the only JSON representation for full bead snapshots and patches.
2) Move/centralize the patch semantics checks (workflow/claim coherence, created_at/created_by rules, etc.) into a single validator: `ValidatedBeadPatch::try_from(BeadPatchWireV1)` in `beads-core`.
3) Update git store wire (`crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs`) to serialize/deserialize via `BeadSnapshotWireV1` (eliminate its bespoke `WireBead` parsing where possible). Keep any legacy quirks as translation adapters local to that module.
4) Update checkpoint import/export and realtime event encoding to use the same `BeadSnapshotWireV1`/`BeadPatchWireV1` types, so snapshots and patches share identical invariants.
5) Update `beads-surface` and `beads-rs` mutation planning to construct `BeadPatchWireV1` directly, then validate once to get `ValidatedBeadPatch`.
6) Delete or shrink duplicate validators (ParsedBeadPatch / validate_wire_patch), replacing them with a single "parse then validate" path.

**Design Notes**
- Keep backward-compatible parsing where needed (legacy git store, go schema), but make those adapters explicit and local so invariants do not leak.
- If the git store wire requires sparse-stamp behavior, model that as a serializer choice on the canonical wire rather than a separate struct.
- Add conversion helpers in core (e.g., `BeadSnapshotWireV1::from_view`) to keep usage uniform.

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Rendering stays in command files; do not move render logic out during this refactor.

notes.author: darin@darinsmcstudio2.lan
notes.content: Rendering stays in command files; do not move render logic out during this refactor.

title: Unify bead wire formats and patch validation

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] `WatermarkRow` cannot be constructed with invalid seq/head combinations.
- [ ] DB load rejects invalid rows before they reach runtime logic.
- [ ] All index users operate on typed watermarks (no raw seq/head pairs).
- [ ] Tests cover row round‑trip and invalid row rejection.
description:
**Problem**
WAL index `WatermarkRow` stores seqs and head hashes as independent fields (`applied_seq`, `applied_head_sha`, `durable_seq`, `durable_head_sha`). This makes illegal combinations representable (seq>0 with head=None; seq=0 with head=Some). The invariant is only enforced later (or not at all).

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/index.rs:210` (WatermarkRow)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/index.rs:821` (loading rows without invariant checks)

**Impact**
Index can hold inconsistent watermarks, which can break replication and durability checks. Compiler cannot enforce the invariant.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Represent watermarks as typed `Watermark<K>` (or a `WireWatermark { seq, head }`) in the index row.

- Replace the four raw fields with:
```rust
struct WatermarkRow {
  namespace: NamespaceId,
  origin: ReplicaId,
  applied: Watermark<Applied>,
  durable: Watermark<Durable>,
}
```
- DB decode builds `Watermark<Applied>`/`Watermark<Durable>` via `Watermark::new`, so invalid seq/head combos fail immediately.
- DB encode decomposes the typed watermark into columns.

This enforces seq/head correctness at the type boundary.
title: Encode WAL index watermark invariants
